title: Master File Local File Transfer Pricing | Virtual Auditor
description: Expert master file local file transfer pricing by IBBI Registered Valuer CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS, CFE) in India. Fixed-fee pricing.
bodyText:
Looking for expert master file local file transfer pricing? Virtual Auditor provides practitioner-grade transfer pricing services in India, led by CA V. Viswanathan — Fellow Chartered Accountant (FCA) | Associate Company Secretary (ACS). We combine deep regulatory expertise with hands-on execution to deliver results within your timeline.

What is Master File Local File Transfer Pricing | Virtual Auditor?

HEADING2: What We Deliver
Transfer pricing study report with benchmarking analysis and ALP determination. Transfer pricing documentation (Master File, Local File, CbCR) as per Rule 10D/10DA/10DB. Functional, Asset, and Risk (FAR) analysis for all international transactions. Benchmarking study using appropriate method (CUP, TNMM, RPM, CPM, PSM). TP audit defence submissions and hearing representation.
HEADING2: Why Choose Virtual Auditor
Virtual Auditor provides end-to-end transfer pricing services — from documentation and benchmarking to audit defence and appellate proceedings. CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS, CFE) handles complex international transactions including intra-group services, royalties, management fees, and financial transactions. Our documentation withstands TPO scrutiny.
With physical offices in Chennai (Spencer Plaza), Bangalore (MG Road), and Mumbai (Goregaon West), we offer both in-person and remote engagement models.
Transfer pricing compliance in India requires documentation under Section 92D read with Rule 10D, benchmarking using one of the six prescribed methods including CUP, RPM, CPM, TNMM, PSM, or Other Method, and defence against TPO adjustments. We handle Master File, Local File, and Country-by-Country Reporting. Our documentation stands up to TPO scrutiny because we anticipate the specific adjustments commonly made in each transaction type.
HEADING2: Our Process
Step 1: Transaction mapping and FAR analysis. Step 2: Selection of Most Appropriate Method (MAM). Step 3: Benchmarking analysis and ALP determination. Step 4: Report preparation compliant with Rule 10D/10DA. Step 5: Filing support and TP audit defence. 
Our transfer pricing documentation includes economic analysis that anticipates common TPO adjustments — working capital adjustments, risk adjustments, comparability filters, and multiple year data analysis. This proactive approach means fewer audit adjustments and stronger defence positions.
HEADING2: Get Started Today
Ready to engage Virtual Auditor for master file local file transfer pricing? Contact us for a free initial consultation:

faqs.q: When is master file local file transfer pricing required?
faqs.a: Transfer pricing documentation is mandatory for entities with international transactions exceeding Rs 1 crore or specified domestic transactions exceeding Rs 20 crores in aggregate. The documentation must be maintained by the due date of filing the return of income.

faqs.q: What is the penalty for not maintaining transfer pricing documentation?
faqs.a: Failure to maintain TP documentation attracts penalty of 2% of the value of international transactions under Section 271AA. Additional penalties apply for non-filing of CbCR (Rs 5,000/day) and failure to furnish information (Rs 50,000/day).

faqs.q: How long does it take to prepare a transfer pricing study?
faqs.a: Typically 2-4 weeks depending on the number and complexity of international transactions. We recommend starting well before the return filing due date. Contact us at +91-99622 60333 for timeline confirmation.
